## Discussion

The system calls this method each time the timer animating the screen saver fires. The time between calls to this method is always at least [doc://com.apple.screensaver/documentation/ScreenSaver/ScreenSaverView/animationTimeInterval]. The system locks focus on your view before it calls this method, so you can use this method to draw content. You can also let [doc://com.apple.screensaver/documentation/ScreenSaver/ScreenSaverView/draw(_:)] perform the drawing, in which case you use this method to call [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/AppKit/NSView/setNeedsDisplay(_:)] to mark your view as dirty. The default implementation of this method does nothing.

# animateOneFrame()

**Instance Method**

Advances the screen saver’s animation by a single frame.

## Declaration

```swift
func animateOneFrame()
```

## Discussion

The system calls this method each time the timer animating the screen saver fires. The time between calls to this method is always at least [doc://com.apple.screensaver/documentation/ScreenSaver/ScreenSaverView/animationTimeInterval]. The system locks focus on your view before it calls this method, so you can use this method to draw content. You can also let [doc://com.apple.screensaver/documentation/ScreenSaver/ScreenSaverView/draw(_:)] perform the drawing, in which case you use this method to call [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/AppKit/NSView/setNeedsDisplay(_:)] to mark your view as dirty. The default implementation of this method does nothing.

## Animating the screen saver

- **startAnimation()**: Activates the periodic timer that animates the screen saver.
- **stopAnimation()**: Deactivates the timer that advances the animation.
- **isAnimating**: A Boolean value that indicates whether the screen saver is animating.
